title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip and Colston engage in a tense discussion regarding the conflicting suspicions surrounding the murder of Mrs. Aigle. Philip notes that Leo suspects Gwendolyn, Gwendolyn suspects Leo, Tina possesses knowledge of the event, Micky knows the truth but remains indifferent, and Mary suspects Hester. Philip accuses Colston of knowing the truth, but Colston warns him that his pursuit is dangerous, specifically noting Philip's physical vulnerability as a former pilot now in a wheelchair. Colston reveals her intention to maintain an official story that blames Jacqueline to avoid further trouble, dismissing Philip's mention of Dr. Calgary's involvement by suggesting a concussion may have altered the facts.

After Colston leaves, Philip considers his attempt to extract information a success. He begins taking notes on potential leads and identifies Tina as his next target. When his wife, Mary, enters and questions him, Philip jokingly claims he is writing to the prosecutor before telling her he is writing to Tina.

In a separate scene, Inspector Huester interviews a young man named Cyril Green regarding the murder of Mrs. Aigle that occurred two years prior. Cyril’s mother interjects to clarify that a "Russian-made satellite" Cyril previously claimed to see was actually a large red bubble car. Cyril recounts that on the evening of the murder, he went out to play with other children on a new path after finishing his tea. Constable Good observes the interaction, noting that the local children often engaged in minor mischief, such as stealing chrysanthemums.
